Job Title: Lead Change and Business Transformation Manager

Salary: Attractive salary plus NFP salary packaging
Ad Text:
Our client is a large, purpose-driven organisation that delivers essential services across Australia and plays an important role in supporting communities. They are currently seeking a Senior Accountant to join their finance team in a role that blends financial accounting with systems, data, and process improvement.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone who enjoys working with large datasets, improving reporting processes, and leveraging tools such as Power BI and Power Query, while still maintaining strong foundations in financial accounting and reporting.

About the Role

Reporting into a senior leader within the finance function, this role will play a key part in strengthening financial reporting processes and enhancing how financial data is captured, analysed, and presented across the organisation.

You will work closely with finance and operational stakeholders to improve reporting capability, automate manual processes, and ensure the integrity of financial information.

Key Responsibilities
Support the month-end process, including reconciliations, journals and maintaining general ledger integrity
Assist with statutory reporting and preparation of financial statements
Analyse large and complex financial datasets to generate insights and improve reporting quality
Develop and enhance Power BI dashboards and automated reporting tools
Use Power Query and other data tools to streamline and automate existing finance processes
Work closely with stakeholders to improve data accuracy, reporting capability, and financial visibility
Identify opportunities to improve systems, reporting structures and finance workflows
About You
CA/CPA qualified preferred
Strong experience in financial accounting and reporting environments
Comfortable working with large datasets and financial systems
Experience with Power BI, Power Query, or similar data visualisation/automation tools
Strong Excel capability and an interest in improving processes through data and technology
Ability to communicate financial insights clearly to non-finance stakeholders
Naturally curious and proactive with a mindset focused on continuous improvement
What’s on Offer
Opportunity to work in a purpose-driven organisation with a strong national presence
A role that sits at the intersection of finance, systems, and data analytics
Exposure to large and complex datasets and reporting environments
Supportive and collaborative finance team
Flexible working arrangements and strong work-life balance

Job Title: Managing Consultant - Data - AI - Gen AI
Ad Text:
About the Role
We are looking for a results-driven Data professional to lead high-performing teams, drive business growth, and deliver value for clients. This leadership position involves overseeing client engagements, mentoring team members, and contributing to strategic business development.

Key Responsibilities

Lead business improvement projects using proven methodologies and tools.

Manage a portfolio.

Build strong client relationships to ensure satisfaction and repeat business.

Mentor teams and drive advancements in AI/ML practices.

Support business development through sales, networking, and proposal creation.

Requirements

At least 10 years in management consulting, with 5+ years in ML/AI applications.

Proven track record in developing and deploying ML models in production environments.

Expertise in ML frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, TensorFlow) and advanced AI technologies.

Experience in delivering large-scale AI/data transformations.

Industry experience in planning, logistics, procurement, or manufacturing is a plus.

Desired Attributes

Collaborative, confident, and a strong leader.

Commercially savvy with a solid sales and business development record.

Skilled communicator and decisive problem solver.

Environmentally conscious and committed to sustainable practices.

Join a leading data and integration consulting firm delivering cloud-based solutions for a range of high-profile clients. The company prides itself on a collaborative culture, flexible working environment, and opportunities to develop advanced technical skills. This role is highly hands-on and consulting-focused, combining data engineering expertise with client engagement and mentoring responsibilities.

Responsibilities

Lead the delivery of data engineering projects, ensuring high-quality outcomes and adherence to best practices.

Build and maintain data pipelines, transformations, and integrations across data lakes, lakehouses, and warehouses.

Apply data modelling principles (star and snowflake schemas) to support analytics and AI/ML workloads.

Mentor and review work of junior and intermediate team members to maintain technical standards.

Collaborate with architects on solution design, including cloud-native and modern engineering patterns.

Implement and maintain DevOps practices, including version control, CI/CD pipelines, and automated testing.

Lead testing, validation, and quality assurance of data solutions.

Serve as the primary technical contact for clients, participating in scoping, estimation, and requirements discussions.

Contribute to continuous improvement of internal processes, templates, and team knowledge sharing.

The requirements

6+ years in data engineering or a related field, with 4+ years hands-on in Databricks (Delta Live Tables, notebooks, workflows).

Strong proficiency in Python and SQL for data transformation and analysis.

Experience with cloud-based data solutions, DevOps practices, and well-architected scalable pipelines.

Familiarity with data modelling (star/snowflake), Medallion architecture, and testing/data quality frameworks.

Knowledge of Microsoft Fabric or similar cloud components a plus.

Experience with data visualisation tools (Power BI or equivalent).

Excellent communication, problem-solving, and consulting skills.

Self-motivated, results-driven, adaptable, and able to manage multiple priorities.

Role - PowerBI Specialist (Short contract)
Duration - 23/03/2026 to 02/04/2026
Location - Brisbane CBD based
Competitive hourly rate
** Open to Brisbane based candidates, PRs/ Citizens only

Short-term Power BI Specialist Contract
Assist a government agency by developing dashboards and data models using Power BI and SQL. This is a very short contract requiring quick delivery of solutions.

Skills for Success:

Strong SQL data extraction, transformation, and validation skills
Ability to quickly understand requirements and deliver practical solutions
Excellent communication and organizational skills
Experience in fast-paced, time-sensitive environments is a plus
Government sector experience is highly regarded

We are seeking a Senior Data Scientist to join an Intelligent Solutions Delivery team, supporting the delivery of AI‑enabled solutions for Claims and enterprise transformation initiatives. This role is focused on building, validating, and productionising AI and machine learning solutions that are embedded directly into business workflows, with a strong emphasis on responsible, governed, and scalable AI delivery.

You will work closely with AI Solution Architects, Engineers, Claims SMEs, and Delivery Squads to develop models and intelligent components using Databricks AI/ML frameworks, Python, and SQL, contributing to agent‑based, predictive, and decision‑support solutions across the claims lifecycle.

Key Responsibilities

Design, build, and iterate machine learning and AI solutions that support Claims transformation initiatives (e.g. triage, summarisation, risk detection, decision support).
Develop analytical and ML components that can be embedded into agentic and AI‑assisted workflows.
Apply appropriate modelling techniques (predictive modelling, NLP, classification, regression, anomaly detection) aligned to business outcomes.
Build and manage models using Databricks AI/ML frameworks, leveraging scalable compute and governed data access.
Use Python for feature engineering, model training, evaluation, and inference logic.
Use SQL to extract, transform, and validate data used in AI and ML pipelines.
Contribute to reusable notebooks, pipelines, and patterns that accelerate AI delivery across squads.
Support the transition of models from experimentation to production, working with engineers on deployment patterns within Databricks.
Implement model documentation, performance tracking, and validation artefacts to support model risk, compliance, and audit requirements.
Monitor model behaviour over time (performance, drift, stability) and recommend improvements.

Skills & Experience (Required)

Proven experience delivering data science or machine learning solutions in a production or enterprise environment.
Strong hands‑on experience with Python for data analysis and ML development.
Experience working with Databricks, including AI/ML workloads and notebook‑based development.
Solid SQL capability for data exploration, feature creation, and validation.
Experience across the ML lifecycle: problem framing, data preparation, modelling, evaluation, and operational handover.
Strong communication skills and ability to work in cross‑functional delivery teams.
Partner with Intelligent Solutions Delivery squads, Solution Architects, and Engineers to ensure AI components align to end‑to‑end solution design.
Collaborate with Claims, Operations, and Risk stakeholders to validate assumptions and ensure models are interpretable and fit‑for‑purpose.

Desirable Experience

Exposure to agentic AI, GenAI, NLP, or AI‑assisted workflows.
Experience working in regulated or risk‑aware environments (e.g. financial services, insurance).
Familiarity with MLOps practices (model versioning, reproducibility, monitoring).
Understanding of ethical AI, explainability, and governance considerations.

Job Title: Principal Consultant - Azure Data Engineer. Brisbane
Ad Text:
Azure Data Engineer - Principal Consultant. Brisbane

About Us

At Agile Insights, we are passionate about empowering organisations to transform through the power of Data & AI. We work with global brands and leading organisations to deliver innovative solutions that solve real business challenges. We have a fantastic, long-standing relationship with Microsoft. We are extremely proud to be a Microsoft Solutions Specialist for four competencies including: Data Analytics and AI, Azure and Digital & App Innovation.

Your Role

We are seeking an experienced and dynamic Azure Data Engineer Principal Consultant to lead data engineering engagements, design scalable data solutions, and help clients unlock the full potential of their data. You will act as a trusted advisor, providing technical leadership and strategic guidance while working on cutting-edge Microsoft Azure technologies.

Key Responsibilities

Technical Leadership

Design and implement scalable, secure, and high-performing data pipelines and data platforms on Microsoft Azure.

Lead solution architecture discussions for Azure-based data engineering solutions, including data lakes, data warehouses, and data integration pipelines.

Advise clients on best practices, governance frameworks, and modern data architecture strategies.

Drive innovation by leveraging Microsoft services such as Microsoft Fabric, Azure Synapse, Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, Azure Data Lake, and Azure Purview.

Technical Management & Delivery

Oversee the end-to-end delivery of data engineering projects, ensuring quality, scope, and timelines are met.

Manage project teams, ensuring efficient task delegation and mentoring team members in Azure tools and techniques.

Collaborate with stakeholders to define technical requirements and deliver solutions aligned with business goals.

Client Engagement

Act as a trusted advisor to clients, providing insights and recommendations for data strategy and architecture.

Conduct workshops, technical demonstrations, and proof-of-concept (POC) projects to showcase Azure's capabilities.

Foster long-term client relationships and identify opportunities for future engagements.

Innovation & Knowledge Sharing

Stay updated on emerging trends in Azure Data and AI technologies and share knowledge with internal teams.

Develop reusable frameworks, accelerators, and best practices to enhance project delivery efficiency.

Contribute to thought leadership initiatives, including whitepapers, blogs, and conference presentations.

Collaborate with Microsoft and other partners to secure funding and co-develop innovative solutions. 

Practice Growth:

Support pre-sales efforts by crafting proposals, developing demos, and estimating project delivery costs. 

Contribute to Agile Insights' thought leadership by participating in industry events, panels, and conferences. 

Build and nurture a network of client relationships, identifying opportunities to expand Agile Insights’ presence in the market. 

Required Skills & Qualifications

Experience:

8+ years of experience in data engineering, with at least 5 years working with Microsoft Azure technologies.

Proven experience in delivering large-scale data solutions for enterprise clients.

Technical Expertise:

Hands-on experience with Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, Azure Data Lake, and Azure SQL Database.

Strong proficiency in data modelling, ETL/ELT processes, and designing scalable data pipelines.

Proficiency in Python, PySpark, SQL, or similar programming languages.

Knowledge of cloud security, compliance, and governance frameworks.

Knowledge on Microsoft Fabric is highly desirable.

Hands-on experience on CI/CD deployments using Azure DevOps.

Familiarity with BI tools like Power BI is a plus.

Certifications (preferred):

Microsoft Certified: Azure Data Engineer Associate

Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect Expert

Microsoft Certified: Fabric Analytics Engineer Associate

Other relevant Azure certifications

Soft Skills:

Strong communication and presentation skills to engage with technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Leadership abilities, including mentoring junior engineers and managing project teams.

Problem-solving mindset with a focus on innovation and client success.

What’s In It for You?

At Agile Insights, we believe in nurturing talent and creating a supportive, flexible, and rewarding workplace. Here’s what you can look forward to:

Wellbeing: Access to hybrid work arrangements, an Employee Assistance Program, and an individualised gym and wellbeing program.

Flexibility: Work from anywhere for up to 6 weeks per year.

Professional Development: Additional paid leave for certifications and exams, with Agile-sponsored training programs for Microsoft and Databricks certifications.

Team Engagement: Enjoy regular team meetups, including state-based gatherings and an annual all-company event.

Equipment & Hardware: Receive an allowance for your home office setup and a 2-year equipment incentive.

Salary Packaging: Access novated leasing services for tax-effective car purchases.

Learning Resources: Expand your skills with access to our digital library and learning platforms. Opportunity to work with some of the best developers in the business including one on one mentoring with senior consultants.

Why Agile Insights?

Work with some of the largest clients and global brands across diverse industries.

Be part of a fast-growing, award-winning team passionate about transforming organisations with Data & AI.

Thrive in a friendly start-up culture that values innovation, collaboration, and work-life balance.

Ready to Lead with Us?

If you’re passionate about analytics, consulting, and community engagement, we’d love to hear from you.

Apply now to join Agile Insights and take your career to the next level!

No Agencies - No overseas applications - Must be in Australia and have full work rights

Security (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
At ASIC, you can be the change that ensures a fair, strong and efficient financial system for the benefit of all Australians. Contribute to delivering on ASIC's purpose, vision, and strategic priorities to help maintain the integrity of the financial system and protect consumers from harm.

 

ASIC's Business Analysis team is currently seeking experienced Business Analysts to join their high-performing team and contribute to a range of strategic Cyber Security, Infrastructure and Data Governance projects.

 

Sydney or Melbourne locations available
24 month fixed term position
Salary from $152,074 to $158,666 (depending on experience) + 15.4 % superannuation
Applications will close at 11:59pm AEDT, Tuesday 17 March 2026 

 

The team 

The Digital, Data and Technology Portfolio Office provides a wide range of project and support services to ASIC. We are undertaking major technology projects that will transform our business and continue our significant technology transformation in several areas such as visualisation, mobility and cloud.

 

The role

As a Cyber Security Business Analyst, you will be responsible for leading key analytical activities to ensure successful project delivery. Responsibilities include high-level and detailed requirements elicitation and management, enterprise analysis, solution assessment, validation and analysis of both functional and non-functional requirements.

 

More specifically, you will: 

Collaborate with business SMEs, Cyber Security team and IT teams to analyse requirements and uncover underlying needs through stakeholder workshops and discussions.
Document functional and non-functional requirements in clear, accessible formats such as business requirements documents or user stories tailored to diverse stakeholders including Cyber Security, Business, Executives, Designers, Developers and Testers.
Rapidly develop an understanding of the technology landscape, business structure and strategic priorities to assess their impact on team deliverables.
Support UAT preparation and system implementation activities, including the creation of user manuals and training materials as required.
Demonstrate strong self-management and accountability in delivering outcomes aligned with ASIC's strategic priorities and team objectives.

 

About you

The ideal candidate this role will be an experienced, proactive and detail-oriented Business Analyst professional who possess strong analytical and communication skills. They will excel at translating complex business needs into clear, actionable requirements and thrive working within a collaborative environment with both business and IT stakeholders.

 

In addition, you will have:

Tertiary qualifications in Information Technology or a related discipline.
Strong understanding of Systems Development Life Cycle (SDLC) and Agile delivery methodologies.
Experience working in large, complex organisations with exposure to cyber security initiatives.
Solid knowledge of security frameworks, concepts and controls.
Demonstrated experience in infrastructure project delivery.
Proven capability in application onboarding, access provisioning and lifecycle management.
Familiarity with data governance platforms and practices.
Experience implementing and managing Privileged Access Management (PAM) solutions.
Understanding of Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) technologies and enterprise integration.
Awareness of AI-powered productivity tools and their application in business environments.
Working knowledge of Governance, Risk and Compliance (GRC) frameworks.

 

Click ‘apply' to start your application. Applications will close at 11:59pm AEDT, Tuesday 17 March 2026.

 

About ASIC

ASIC's remit is one of the broadest of regulators across the world. ASIC regulates corporations, markets, financial services and consumer credit and monitors and promotes market integrity and consumer protection in the Australian financial system. Through our enforcement work, we hold to account those who contravene the law, working to achieve strong outcomes that address the greatest consumer and investor harms. 

ASIC is an equal opportunity employer seeking people who want to make a difference. ASIC is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace where the very best talent in Australia chooses to work. Applications from people of all ages, cultural backgrounds, abilities, LGBTIQ+ identity, and people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander decent are encouraged to apply.

 

We offer a range of employee benefits including:

Attractive superannuation
Additional leave entitlements
50/50 hybrid work-from home model
Flexible work arrangements
Assistance for study and professional development

 

Click here to view ASIC's salary and benefits guide.

To read more about ASIC, you can visit our website or review our Corporate Plan.

To work with us, you need to be an Australian citizen and be prepared to complete an ASIC Suitability and Baseline Assessment.

About the role – Full-time, 12-month fixed term contract

Be part of our transformation journey and join us as Senior Delivery Lead and help shape the digital banking experience that powers our members every day.

Lead the delivery of large, complex strategic initiatives that drive meaningful transformation and value across the organisation. Partner closely with Product Owners and senior stakeholders to turn strategy into outcomes, while enabling high-performing, self-organising teams to deliver with quality, pace and impact. This is an opportunity to shape how change is delivered across a major transformation portfolio while mentoring and uplifting delivery capability.

Key responsibilities include:  

·        Leading the delivery of large and complex strategic initiatives within a program/value stream, ensuring prioritised backlogs and technical projects are delivered with quality, pace and measurable business value.

·        Partnering closely with the Product Owner and business stakeholders to translate strategy into delivery outcomes, ensuring initiatives drive value creation, business adoption and successful value realisation.

·        Creating high-performing, self-organising delivery teams by mentoring delivery resources, removing blockers, and fostering a culture of collaboration, continuous improvement and delivery excellence.

·        Driving end-to-end project execution including planning, sprint/iteration management, dependency coordination, risk and issue management, and deployment activities across Agile, Waterfall or hybrid environments.

·        Building strong executive and stakeholder relationships, maintaining transparent communication with business owners, program leadership and sponsors on progress, risks, financials and outcomes.

·        Ensuring disciplined governance and financial oversight, managing budgets, forecasts, quality controls and cross-program dependencies to optimise delivery outcomes across the broader change portfolio.

About you 

You will succeed in this role if you have:

·        Extensive project delivery experience with a proven track record managing large, complex strategic initiatives within enterprise environments.

·        Strong expertise across Agile, Waterfall or hybrid delivery methodologies, with the ability to adapt approaches to maximise delivery outcomes.

·        Exceptional stakeholder engagement and influencing skills, with the confidence to work closely with Product Owners, executives and cross-functional teams.

·        Ability to lead and mentor delivery teams, fostering high-performing, self-organising teams and promoting a culture of collaboration and continuous improvement.

·        Strong problem-solving and analytical mindset, comfortable navigating complexity, managing competing priorities and removing delivery blockers.

·        Sound financial and governance capability, including managing budgets, forecasts, risks, issues and dependencies across multiple initiatives.

·        Experience delivering change within financial services or similarly regulated environments, with an understanding of business, technology and operational interdependencies.

·        A proactive, delivery-focused mindset, committed to driving quality outcomes, business adoption and measurable value realisation.

Location

Whilst our primary office locations are in Adelaide and Brisbane, we are pleased to be able to consider applications from employees within Australia for this role. We also encourage you to put thought into how the work may be undertaken given your location and what support you may require.

Why you’ll love working with us: 

People First Bank – our new brand – says exactly what we’re all about: people. We're committed to supporting and growing our team and creating an outstanding work environment.

You’ll have access to financial and lifestyle benefits to support your success and individuality:

Flexibility and hybrid working arrangements.

Enhance your salary with discounts on banking, health insurance, and shopping across Australia.

Advance your career with training, study support, and project opportunities.

Competitive leave benefits that empower employees to take time for themselves and loved ones.

Wellbeing support with our Employee Assistance Program, wellbeing leave, mental health app, and mental health ambassadors’ program.

Make a difference with paid volunteering, Workplace Giving, and diverse community initiatives.

Be recognised for your contributions through our peer-driven recognition program.

 A bit about People First Bank:  

In March 2023, People’s Choice and Heritage Bank merged to form Heritage and People’s Choice Limited - Australia's leading mutual bank.  We are now on an exciting journey to bring together our products, services, and technology under a single brand - People First Bank.

We have over 700,000 members and 1,900 employees, with dual head offices in Adelaide and Toowoomba, an Australian-based contract centre and branches across five states. 

With a vision ‘To deliver positive change through banking’, People First Bank serves members and contributes to the community, while offering an inclusive, flexible work environment. We’re big enough to make an impact; but agile enough to influence and do interesting work.

Apply online now:

If this sounds like you, this could be the next step in your career. Click 'Apply' now and submit your application, including a cover letter and current resume by COB Tuesday, 24th March 2026.

We are committed to diversity and inclusion and support candidate requests for adjustments to enable everyone to equitably participate in our selection process.

About The Role
Financial Specialist required for a fixed term contract for an initial 18 month project assisting the finance area to implement Dynamics 365. This role will be instrumental in providing hands on guidance from design through to go live and go live support. You will be the SME withing this space advising between finance and the business.

Responsibilities will include:
• Lead finance input into the Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations (F&O) implementation, partnering with the ERP Lead and external implementation partners.
• Provide financial and accounting expertise to ensure system design meets accounting standards, reporting requirements and project operations needs.
• Work with finance, project and operational teams to define and document future state workflows for F&O and Project Operations, driving standardisation and best practice outcomes.
• Design and embed robust internal controls and approval workflows, including segregation of duties, approval hierarchies and automated controls.
• Ensure the system supports statutory, management and tax reporting requirements, working with Financial Assurance to validate governance, audit trails and controls.
• Lead financial data cleansing and migration activities, ensuring accuracy, reconciliation and data integrity across legacy and new systems.
• Coordinate finance testing activities, including UAT, developing test scenarios and supporting issue resolution.
• Support change and adoption through training materials and hands on guidance for finance users.

What you’ll bring to the table:
• 8+ years’ experience in finance or accounting roles.
• Proven ERP implementation experience, including configuring or supporting Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations.
• Strong understanding of accounting principles, internal controls, compliance and audit requirements.
• Demonstrated experience supporting data migration, system testing and user training during ERP implementations.
• Ability to collaborate effectively with both finance and technical teams, translating business requirements into system solutions.
• Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to work closely with finance teams, IT specialists, project managers, vendors and non financial stakeholders.
• Proven capability to bridge the gap between technical teams and finance users to ensure shared understanding and successful outcomes.
• Comprehensive understanding of end to end finance processes, including purchasing, invoicing, revenue recognition and financial close.
• Strong appreciation of how finance processes impact broader business operations.
• Ability to align system configuration with strategic objectives, delivering transparency, insight and business value beyond transactional processing.
• Related degree or CPA/CA

The Opportunity

An exciting opportunity to join a leading general insurer in Australia and New Zealand, recognised for its strong market position, collaborative culture and investment in data-driven decision making.

This role sits within a high-performing FP&A function and will partner closely with senior leaders across the business to drive financial performance and commercial outcomes. You will also lead a small team of analysts, providing both technical and people leadership.

Key Responsibilities
Partner with senior stakeholders to provide commercial insights and financial advice to support strategic decision making
Lead and develop a team of 1–2 Finance Business Partnering analysts
Deliver detailed insurance performance analysis, including Loss Ratio and NCOR, identifying trends and opportunities to improve profitability
Present financial performance and insights to senior leadership, including recommendations and commentary
Lead budgeting and forecasting across P&L, Balance Sheet and Cash Flow
Support Board and Committee reporting, including performance analysis and competitor insights
Manage legal entity reporting, including transfer pricing and reconciliation between management and statutory results

About Francom

Financial freedom is the Australian dream. At our core, we exist to help people regain financial control when the system has stopped feeling fair, navigable, or humane. We step into moments of imbalance - where power, information or options are uneven and apply discipline, integrity and judgement to reach outcomes that are sustainable, lawful and human. Join us with your experience as a senior data engineer to apply systems and data thinking that will help restore agency when people need clarity, fairness and a way forward. At Francom our values drive every part of the work we do from ideation, to implementation. Our systems, processes and technology enable our operations.
Our work focuses on resolution over enforcement, and progress over pressure. Financial freedom means enabling people to move forward with dignity. We’re seeking senior data engineers to design and build the data foundations that support real-time analytics, and decision-making.

The Role

We are seeking a Senior Data Engineer to provide technical leadership within our data team. This role is responsible for designing scalable, secure, and reliable data infrastructure while setting best practice standards across data engineering, governance, and delivery.
The Senior Data Engineer will take end-to-end ownership of complex data pipelines and architectures, mentor junior team members, and partner closely with business and technical stakeholders to deliver trusted, high-quality data solutions that support strategic and operational decision-making.

Key responsibilities

Lead the design, build and optimisation of enterprise-grade data pipelines, ELT processes and CI/CD workflows using Azure Data Factory, DBT and Azure Databricks
Architect, build and govern curated and aggregated data layers to support analytics, reporting and regulatory requirements
Develop and optimise complex SQL workloads, ensuring scalability, performance and data integrity
Use Python to support advanced data transformation, orchestration and automation
Build and maintain integrations across data warehouses, real-time streaming platforms, databases, ETL tools, governance platforms and BI tools
Data quality, governance and security

Ensure data integrity across the modern data platform, including accuracy, consistency and completeness
Champion strong data governance, quality, security and documentation practices
Secure data at rest and in transit through appropriate encryption and access controls
Create and publish data flow diagrams, data models and system documentation
Partner with analysts and business users to build and maintain the enterprise data catalogue
Delivery, operations and support

Translate business requirements into robust, compliant and scalable data solutions
Support platform enhancements, capacity planning, performance monitoring and incident resolution
Provide second-level support on a rotational roster
Work closely with the broader team to meet delivery commitments and operational targets
Leadership and collaboration

Provide technical leadership, guidance and mentoring to data engineers and analysts
Drive continuous improvement in engineering practices, delivery quality and team engagement
Act in accordance with Francom’s risk, compliance and values frameworks, setting a high standard for accountability and professional conduct
About You

Tertiary qualification in engineering, mathematics, computer science, or a related discipline.
4-7 years of experience in building and maintaining enterprise grade data infrastructure
Advanced technical capability in SQL, Python, Git, and CI/CD pipelines
Extensive hands-on experience with Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, Azure DevOps, Power BI, and GitHub
Deep understanding of ELT/ETL architectures, data warehousing, and modern data platforms
Proven experience designing and governing aggregated data models in production environments
Experience in transforming and manipulating large and complex datasets. Transactional and financial data and banking/ payments experience highly regarded
Strong problem-solving, decision-making, and stakeholder engagement skills
Experienced with SQL modern data stores, able to write complex queries and transformations.
Demonstrable track record of dealing well with ambiguity, prioritizing needs, and delivering results in a dynamic environment.
Full Australian working rights
Champion a culture of innovation, experimentation, and continuous improvement across the data platform.
Can do attitude
Nice to have:

Familiarity with data reconciliation, PII and PCI concepts for working with payment card and transaction data.
Good understanding of data profiling, query design
Extensive knowledge of cloud scripting (Python) in one or more data processing technologies
Familiar with APIs to pull data from various systems/platforms.
Experience with security, networking, and compliance best practices across the platform.
Support ML pipeline integrations and AI-focused data initiatives.
Software engineering or DevOps experience (e.g. CI/CD, source control, infrastructure as code)
What We Offer

A senior role with real influence in a values-driven organisation
The opportunity to lead, mentor, and shape Francom’s data engineering capability
A professional, collaborative, and accountable culture investing in community impact and positioning for growth
Modern Parramatta office location where some travel may be required.
Free monthly lunches, perks, acknowledgements and birthday celebrations
Competitive salary package aligned to seniority, experience, and contribution
Please note: This role is office-based and is not available on a remote or hybrid basis.

Ad Text:
Vice President (Operations) – 15‑Month Max Term Contract

QIC Private Equity | Brisbane

A rare opportunity to step into a senior operational leadership role within one of Australia’s most respected global investment managers.

QIC’s Private Equity (QPE) team is offering a 15‑month maternity leave contract for a highly capable Vice President focused on Operations. This role is ideal for an investment operations leader ready to take ownership of a global, complex portfolio and drive operational excellence in a fast‑growing asset class.

About the Role

As Vice President, you will oversee the end‑to‑end operational, data and reporting platform, supporting a global private equity program with more than 3,000 underlying companies across funds, co‑investments, secondaries and direct equity investments.

You’ll be the engine room of QPE’s operations, uplifting systems, strengthening governance, elevating data insight, and ensuring reporting and performance narratives exceed client expectations.

You will lead a small, high‑performing team and act as a client‑facing representative of QPE. The role is diverse and hands‑on, with exposure across:

Operational leadership and uplift

eFront platform ownership

Forecasting, reporting and analytics

Valuation assurance and audit oversight

Post‑investment portfolio monitoring

Client reporting and presentations

Partnering with QPE’s global team, and internal QIC teams

This is a max‑term contract, enabling a generous handover period with the incumbent and offering a unique development opportunity in a growing asset class.

About You

In this role, you will draw on a genuine enjoyment of solving complex problems and bringing structure and clarity to fast‑moving environments. 

A background in funds management, particularly within private equity or venture capital operations or fund accounting, combined with strong data and reporting capabilities, will provide a strong foundation. It will be important to stay composed under pressure, work through detail while keeping sight of the bigger picture and offer insights that others can rely on.

You will bring a global outlook and a natural sense of ownership to your work. Because the role involves engaging with sophisticated stakeholders, being comfortable having informed, honest conversations, and explaining complex information in a clear, approachable way, will make a meaningful difference. Reputation is established from consistently delivering thoughtful, accurate, and dependable work.

You will also play a key role in leading and supporting a capable, small hardworking team. An approachable and fair leadership style, with a focus on providing constructive feedback and creating opportunities for growth, will help the team thrive.

This position suits someone who values improving processes, strengthening systems and creating an environment where people can do their best work.

Your Responsibilities

In this role, you will:

Drive Operational Excellence

Lead process optimisation, data uplift and workflow improvements across QPE.

Champion adoption and enhancement of QPE’s core systems, particularly eFront.
 

Own the Data & Reporting Platform

Maintain a trusted, scalable data environment for investment reporting and analysis.

Coordinate quarterly investment reviews and client reporting, ensuring high‑quality, reconciled, mandate‑compliant outputs.
 

Strengthen Governance & Assurance

Support valuations and lead assurance activities.

Drive insight‑rich reporting that enhances transparency and decision‑making.
 

Lead Portfolio Monitoring & Forecasting

Oversee cash forecasting, fee modelling and performance analytics across the portfolio.

Drive comprehensive portfolio‑level monitoring.
 

Provide Senior Client & Stakeholder Engagement

Represent QPE in client meetings and reporting discussions.

Partner with Investment teams, Client Services, Finance, IT, and external providers.
 

Lead & Develop a Team

Manage, mentor and develop two high‑performing analysts.

Essential Criteria

To be successful, you will bring:

Tertiary qualifications in Commerce, Finance or Accounting

Postgraduate qualifications such as CA, CFA or CAIA (highly regarded)

Experience in Private Equity or Venture Capital and their fund structures, portfolio monitoring and operational processes

Global experience preferred

Strong operational background, particularly in system enhancement and process re‑engineering

Proven experience partnering with IT and data teams

Client‑facing capability with institutional clients

Exceptional attention to detail, high accountability, resilience and a strong ownership mindset

Experience using eFront or similar private markets platforms

Why Join QIC?

QIC is a global investment manager with over A$100 billion in assets under management. You will join a purpose‑led organisation that delivers shared value for clients, communities and people.

Within QPE, you will be part of a collaborative, growing team working in one of the most exciting private markets portfolios in Australia—with exposure to international offices including San Francisco and Copenhagen.

You’ll also benefit from:

A generous handover period

Working with a leader invested in team development

A high‑performance culture where insight and innovation are valued

Ready to Apply

If you’re a motivated operational leader seeking a rare opportunity to step into a senior role within a global private equity team, we’d love to hear from you.

This is an exceptional chance to continue driving QPE’s operational excellence—while gaining experience across a diversified and high‑growth private markets platform.

Job Title: Principal Business Analyst - 12 Month Max Term Contract
Ad Text:
A great opportunity to work with one of Australia’s largest general insurers 

Lead capability development by mentoring and uplifting AI capability

Open to Sydney candidates only - Hybrid Flexible Opportunity

About Hollard

Hollard Insurance is part of the Hollard Group of Companies and one of largest General Insurer in Australia and New Zealand. We underwrite a broad range of general insurance products, including motor, home, contents, business, bicycle and pet, in proud partnership with some of Australia's biggest consumer brands and broker businesses. As an independent and privately owned company, we believe we can go further together – for our people, customers, communities and partners – through an innovative approach to providing insurance. 

Our state-of-the-art facilities in North Sydney reflect our commitment to providing innovative workspaces that support hybrid and remote work. 

Your Team

You will be part of a multi-disciplinary Technology team, working closely with business, software vendors and partners to develop, implement and maintain systems that support customers through all aspects of the insurance value chain.

Your Role

Reporting to the Business Analysis & Change Practice Manager, you will be the Practice’s technical expert and most senior BA practitioner.

This role is focused on three things: upholding and evolving BA quality standards across the portfolio, leading BA activities during concept phases, and driving the Practice’s AI capability through hands-on tool development.

You will work alongside BAs at all levels — sharing expertise, role-modelling best practice, and contributing to Practice sessions — with people leadership and career development remaining with the Practice Manager

Your duties and responsibilities

Provide technical coaching and skills development to 45 Business Analysts across all capability levels (Junior to Lead BA), focusing on core BA skills including requirements elicitation, stakeholder analysis and engagement, process mapping, user story writing, impact assessment, and effective use of BA tools and templates

Conduct capability assessments to identify skill gaps and develop targeted coaching interventions, training materials, workshops, and knowledge-sharing sessions on BA methodologies, tools, and emerging industry practices.

Co-facilitate BA Practice sessions and uplift programmes to enhance technical capability, share learnings, and embed best practices across the Practice.

Lead the BA Practice’s AI capability uplift by designing and building AI-powered tools, prompt libraries, artefact templates, and workflow automations using Hollard-approved tools (including Claude and Microsoft Copilot). Act as the Practice’s hands-on AI innovator, translating emerging AI capabilities into practical, reusable assets that enhance productivity and quality across the cohort.

Establish and monitor quality gates at key milestones, ensuring adherence to Practice standards and governance across all projects

Collaborate closely with the Business Analysis & Change Practice Manager, EPMO, delivery teams, and business stakeholders to drive successful project outcomes.

About You

Demonstrated experience in delivering Business Analysis activities across the full delivery lifecycle (concept, planning, delivery, implementation), including project scoping, process modelling, and requirements management.

Strong coaching and mentoring skills to technically develop business analysts at various capability levels, with a track record of successfully uplifting BA technical capability.

Must have experience within Agile delivery environments, large enterprise programmes, and technology transformation initiatives

Good Industry/business knowledge of the Australian Insurance market, with demonstrated experience in General Insurance and understanding of APRA regulatory requirements and their implications for business analysis activities

Demonstrated hands-on experience developing AI-powered tools, templates, and workflow automations — not just using AI tools but building with them.

Experience using large language models (such as Claude, ChatGPT, or Copilot) to develop practical BA assets including prompt libraries, AI-assisted artefact templates, and automated BA workflows.

Working at Hollard 

At Hollard, you’ll discover an environment where you can truly make an impact. We offer the best of both worlds – the opportunity, agility and creativity of a company growing with scale, alongside a focus on becoming a catalyst for enduring, positive, social change. 

We’re a tight-knit team, where authentic voices and unique perspectives matter. We work supportively, collaboratively and inclusively to help shape our future and create shared value for our communities. 

Hollard proudly promotes flexible work including remote work, non-traditional work patterns, and work-family balance. We’re also unapologetically committed to creating a workplace that values your diversity with a true sense of inclusion.  We actively seek to hire for your different lived experiences, including as it relates to disability, ethnicity, faith, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples, gender diversity, sexual orientation, age, neurodiversity. 

We’re building something special. Working closely with our industry partners to reshape insurance for the better, we’re people changing insurance. 

Please note:  Even if you don’t meet all our selection criteria, we encourage you to apply.  

A full background check will be completed on the successful applicant. 

We will not require any support from Recruitment Agencies and kindly request that no unsolicited resumes are sent to Hollard in relation to this role or others. Only direct applications will be considered. 

Visit the Hollard careers page to learn about how Hollard will collect, store and handle your personal information.

Initial 12-month contract
Previous experience working on AI automation project required
Location: CBD- Hybrid work model
 
Our client is looking for a Senior Business Analyst with previous experience working on AI automation initiatives. Working closely with business stakeholders you will document end-to-end  processes, uncover pain points and translate those processes and data into structured inputs suitable for AI-enabled automation and optimisation.

Essential skills and Experience:

Strong business analysis fundamentals (problem definition, root cause analysis)
Experience documenting operational processes
Ability to work with non-technical staff
Strong process mapping capability
Understanding of process-to-data relationships
Experience in asset-heavy regulated environments
Experience translating processes into automation or AI-enabled solutions
Understanding of AI applied to workflow optimisation and decision support
 
For more info contact Aparna on Aparna.sharma@peoplebank.com.au
 


Peoplebank and Leaders IT are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where everyone belongs. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. If you need adjustments to the recruitment process due to your circumstances, please let us know—we’re here to support you.

About Icon Water

Icon Water is an iconic Canberra business with a history of providing essential services to Canberra for over 100 years.

We are the ACT’s supplier of essential water and wastewater services and have proudly served our community for over 100 years – sourcing, treating and supplying water, and managing Canberra’s wastewater network. Our work is essential to the health, prosperity and liveability of the region, and we are committed to environmental, social and financial sustainability in everything we do.

Icon Water is a purpose driven organisation where everyone contributes to our core business, ensuring high quality drinking water and sustainable wastewater treatment. Icon Water’s culture is driven by our values of care, connection, community and courage. These are values we see in action every day as we challenge ourselves to meet the expectations of our community.  




An exciting 6 month opportunity exists to join our People, Health and Safety Group as the HR Systems and Reporting Advisor, where you will be the technical expert and system owner for our Aurion HRMIS core modules and enterprise HR reporting environment. In this pivotal role, you will lead end‑to‑end system administration, configuration, and governance of HR master data and workflows. You will also design, build and deliver high‑quality HR analytics using Power BI.

Key Responsibilities

Oversee the day‑to‑day administration of Aurion HR core modules, including the reporting query tool, organisation structure, position management, establishment control, HR records, and other relevant configuration in partnership with Payroll.
Maintain HR master data to ensure accurate integrations across all Icon Water systems that rely on HR information.
Partner with the IT Applications Support team to manage system upgrades, patches, environment refreshes and release deployments. Coordinate stakeholders, ensure infrastructure readiness, and work with vendors to resolve defects, optimise performance and address service requests.
Use Aurion’s reporting and query tools to set up, maintain and create required reports and dashboards. Build and manage ETL pipelines that ensure accurate and timely data flow into the reporting environment.
Deliver all reporting requirements on time, including commentary and variance analysis, and manage ad hoc reporting requests with documented logic.
Maintain strong reporting governance, including metric definitions, single‑source‑of‑truth frameworks, version control, access controls, and change logs—while driving continuous improvement.
Collaborate with Payroll, Finance, IT and People & Culture teams to align system configuration and reporting with business needs. Provide training and coaching to stakeholders on system usage and dashboard interpretation.

About You

You bring extensive experience using Aurion and hold a relevant degree or equivalent experience in workforce planning, commerce, IT, HR, project management, scheduling, finance or analytics. You also have:

Proven ability to proactively deliver high‑quality, solutions‑focused workforce planning or HR systems support to a diverse client group, including senior managers.
Experience leading organisational change management initiatives.
Strong strategic and holistic thinking, including the ability to anticipate and quantify costs, benefits and impacts of different courses of action.
Ability to integrate people data from multiple HR sources and channels.
Strong relationship‑building skills and the ability to establish credibility with key stakeholders.

To find out more about the role you can view the detailed Position Description on the Icon water Careers Portal.

You can also contact sarah.bowerman@iconwater.com.au via email for further information.

If you’re ready to make a real impact and be part of a company that values your expertise, apply now.

Our client is a global leader providing goods and services to various industries, including mining, construction, and transport. Currently undergoing a restructure of the Procurement function, our client is looking to elevate the business unit to deliver procurement activities, driving further value and cost-saving initiatives.

The Position

Key responsibilities include:

Analyse, extract, cleanse, and interpret supply chain and procurement data.
Supply accurate and timely reports to the procurement department and other key stakeholders.

About the Role

As Senior Data Scientist, you will provide the deep technical expertise and theoretical rigor required to build TEG's next generation of AI products. Supporting the Principal and Lead Data Scientists, you will bridge the gap between cutting-edge research and production-ready applications. Partnering with ML Engineers and Product teams, you will ensure technical excellence through the implementation of production-grade CI/CD, Dockerisation, and robust model monitoring. About you

Minimum 5 years experience in delivering high-impact data science projects from inception to production, ideally within a high-volume e-commerce or digital environment.
A tertiary degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related discipline.
Significant theoretical and practical experience in at least two of our core delivery areas: recommendation engines, pricing optimisation, or customer lifecycle modeling. Proven experience building GenAI and Agentic AI solutions is a plus.
Proven ability to design experimental frameworks and apply statistical methods to validate model performance and business uplift.
Advanced skills in BigQuery and Python, and hands-on experience with GCP and Vertex AI.
Extensive experience working in a collaborative software development environment, including version control (Git) and CI/CD pipelines.
Possess the ability to communicate technical concepts and experimental outcomes to stakeholders at all levels.

About the team

 

As the way Australians shop continues to evolve, our Data and AI Team keep Coles at the forefront of modern retail. We combine data, artificial intelligence, and applied engineering to reimagine how our stores, supply chain, and digital channels operate — creating smarter, more connected, and seamless experiences for our customers and team members.

We serve every part of Coles — from computer vision applications that enhance in-store experiences and reduce loss, to forecasting, optimisation, and decision-support solutions that drive efficiency, accuracy, and agility across our supply chain, retail operations, and corporate functions.

 

The scale and impact of this work are significant. Coles serves millions of customers each week across more than a thousand stores nationwide, and our AI and data capabilities provide the intelligence that underpins how we plan, move, sell, and serve. By combining deep data capability with applied AI and modern engineering, we’re enabling Coles to become a truly AI-enabled enterprise, transforming how we operate and how Australia shops every day.

 



About the role

 

As the AI Value Manager for our Data and Artificial Intelligence team, primary purpose of this position will be to will be to establish and operate an enterprise capability to identify, define, track and realise business value from AI initiatives across Coles. This role ensures AI use cases have clear, credible business cases, well-defined measurement and attribution approaches, and an ongoing value tracking cadence that supports prioritisation and investment decisions.

The role partners closely with Finance, HR productivity teams, and Technology (product and engineering) to translate technical delivery into measurable commercial outcomes, and to ensure benefits are realised in practice — not just projected.

 

 

Key responsibilities will include:

 

Value framework and standards (enterprise)

 

Design and implement an AI value capability, which includes establishing common approaches for:

 

benefits identification and categorisation (financial and non-financial)
baselining, measurement plans and attribution logic
business case quality standards (assumptions, sensitivity, risks, dependencies)

 

Build stakeholder capability by educating leaders and teams on what “good” looks like for AI value measurement and commercial decisioning.

 

Business case development and challenge

 

Partner with initiative owners to develop credible business cases for AI use cases, including:

investment rationale and value drivers
quantified benefits and assumptions
delivery and adoption dependencies
risks, mitigations and measurement approach 

 

Measurement approach and attribution support

 

Work with engineering, product and analytics teams to ensure statistical measurement and instrumentation plans to support objective benefit attribution (e.g., A/B testing approaches, control groups, baselines).

Translate experimental design and results into clear commercial narratives for non-technical audiences.

Ensure consistent interpretation of results, including confidence, limitations, and appropriate claims of value.

 

 

AI cost structure and unit economics

 

Develop a clear understanding of the cost structure of AI use cases, including:

platform and infrastructure costs
licensing/vendor arrangements

 

 

Portfolio reporting and governance

 

Establish portfolio-level reporting of AI benefits, including progress, realised value, forecasts, confidence levels, and key risks/issues.

Build reporting assets (e.g., Power BI dashboards, standard packs) that support Finance reporting rhythms and senior leadership decision-making.

 

Value realisation and issue escalation

 

Maintain an enterprise view of where value is being realised (or not), and proactively identify:

initiatives not demonstrating value
measurement gaps or weak attribution

 

 

About You and Your Skills

 

Strong commercial and financial analysis skills, including ability to build and interrogate business cases and value models.
Ability to understand AI and technology concepts well enough to engage effectively with engineering/product teams (without being a hands-on engineer).
Strong communication skills: able to translate technical outcomes into clear, decision-ready commercial insights for senior stakeholders.
Structured thinking and governance mindset: builds repeatable standards, processes, and reporting cadences.
Comfortable operating in ambiguity and building greenfield capability.

 

Skills and Qualifications

 

Background in one or more of: commercial/finance analyst, strategy consulting, transformation, data/analytics value management, product finance, or investment evaluation.
Experience working with measurement approaches such as A/B tests or other experimental/quasi-experimental methods, and interpreting results for business audiences.
Familiarity with technology commercial models: licensing, cloud cost drivers, and “unit economics” approaches for scaling digital/AI solutions.
Tools: strong Excel modelling; Power BI reporting; working knowledge of SQL to self-serve analysis where needed.

 

 

Please note that our ideal candidate will work with high trust and high influence role with senior stakeholder exposure. They will require strong partnership orientation across Finance, HR and Technology and operates as an individual contributor, with scope to grow as the capability scales.

Unlock Your Potential as a Category Finance Manager

The Category Finance Manager is the gatekeeper of the commercial cluster P&L of the ANZ portfolio. Key responsibilities

Delivering the Year 

Preparing and analysing actual and forecasted performance using variance analysis to arrive at root causes and action plans 

Work cross-functionally with Sales and Trade Finance to vet financials of customer-facing opportunities and assess trade spend issues 

Act as the gatekeeper of the cluster P&L by challenging the sales team to optimize financial results and paybacks, develop and execute plans to control supply chain losses, costing analysis, and oversee portfolio initiatives such as new product development projects

Add value to the business by ensuring cross-functional team receives timely information and understand its implication to support great business decisions 

Corporate Reporting

Support the monthly forecasting process ensuring that forecasts loaded by the ANZ sales team at cluster/category level are sensible vs. plan and prior periods 

Support the annual budget process and presentation for the portfolio  

Planning the Future 

Preparing inputs for and analysing financial forecast, Strategic Plan and Annual Operating Plan including zero based budgeting   

Own the business cases for new products and renovation/innovation providing a financial view and coordinating inputs across the functions 

Work cross-functionally with Marketing in development of innovation P&Ls 

Work cross-functionally with Operations to enable complete business reporting and understanding
